meta.title: How complex Mexico's economy is
meta.source: Harvard Growth Lab, Atlas of Economic Complexity (ECI ranks & 2034 growth projections); export values from the Observatory of Economic Complexity (OEC) / UN COMTRADE
meta.note: Economic complexity = how much productive knowhow a country's exports embody — not how much it sells, but how hard-to-make the things it sells are. The Atlas ranks 145 countries. Export dollar values are 2024 goods trade (OEC / UN COMTRADE, HS 4-digit).

eci.note: Mexico is more complex than its income predicts, and ranks ahead of every Latin American peer. It rose ~2 places over the decade (≈29th to 27th of 145). The endpoints — ≈29th a decade ago and 27th in 2024 — are read from the Atlas 'Economic Complexity over time' chart; the intermediate years trace that chart's gentle upward line and are approximate. Note: OEC's separate, larger ranking universe places Mexico ~26th; we use the Atlas 145-country ranking throughout for consistency.

projection2034.note: Growth projected at ~2.5%/yr through 2034 — only the top half of countries. The Atlas flags this as slow given Mexico's complexity, partly because its export basket has been getting less diversified even as it gets more complex.
